h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to follow the tutor's instructions to identify the beat unit and draw the corresponding notes.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [draws an eighth note under each of the three tiles, stopping at the bar line] Three marks in this bar, lower box takes 8 from the card.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully performs the new task of drawing the correct musical notes under the tiles based on the time signature.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor maintains a consistent scaffolding strategy throughout the dialogue without needing a reversal.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: [draws an eighth note under each of the three tiles, stopping at the bar line] Three marks in this bar, lower box takes 8 from the card.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner successfully performs the new task of drawing the correct musical notes under the tiles based on the time signature.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor maintains a consistent scaffolding strategy throughout the dialogue without needing a reversal.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor maintains a consistent scaffolding strategy throughout the dialogue without needing a reversal.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to place the cards on the diagram following the tutor's step-by-step instructions.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: The up arrow is track on cart. Track card goes there; the track pushes up on the cart.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner independently identifies the force acting on the up arrow and explains its direction, applying the rule of naming the acting object first.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The learner does not exhibit any resistance or breakdown, and the tutor does not need to change their strategy.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no recognition-contingent tutor uptake.
